---
title: "ACCEPT Lab"
body-classes: home
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
<p class="hero-kicker">School of Psychological Sciences | Victoria University of Wellington | Centre for Applied Cross-Cultural Research</p>
<h1 class="hero-title">ACCEPT Lab</h1>
<p class="hero-subtitle">Research on causal questions in culture and social life</p>
<p class="hero-lede">ACCEPT Lab is a research group in the School of Psychological Sciences at Victoria University of Wellington.</p>
<p class="hero-text">We study causal questions in psychology and the social sciences, with particular interests in culture, religion, and social life. Our work includes observational and experimental analysis, teaching and supervision, and open research infrastructure.</p>
<div class="hero-actions">
<a href="research.qmd" class="btn btn-primary">Research</a>
<a href="https://www.placesmap.org/" class="btn btn-secondary">Places Map</a>
<a href="mailto:joseph.bulbulia@vuw.ac.nz" class="btn btn-secondary">Get in touch</a>
</div>
:::

::: {.home-proof-grid}
::: {.home-proof-item}
**Based at** the [School of Psychological Sciences](https://www.wgtn.ac.nz/psyc)
:::
::: {.home-proof-item}
**Affiliated with** the [Centre for Applied Cross-Cultural Research (CACR)](https://www.wgtn.ac.nz/cacr)
:::
::: {.home-proof-item}
**Methods** observational and experimental causal inference
:::
::: {.home-proof-item}
**Infrastructure** [Places Map](https://www.placesmap.org/) for reproducible spatial and cultural analysis
:::
:::

## Research

ACCEPT Lab studies questions of cause and effect in psychology and the social sciences, with particular interests in culture, religion, and social life. The lab examines how interventions, institutions, and social environments affect behaviour and wellbeing across settings.

## Areas of research

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card .home-card}
### Causal inference

We use causal inference and related methods where they help clarify what changes what, for whom, and under which conditions.

[Four methods papers](resources.qmd#start-here-methods-in-causal-inference-series)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card .home-card}
### Culture and religion

We study how belief, practice, community structure, and cultural setting shape exposure to interventions and responses across groups.

[Research overview](research.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card .home-card}
### Social and policy applications

Current work includes social inequality, volunteering and charity, institutional trust, political psychology, personality, health and wellbeing, meaning and purpose, spirituality, and religion.

[Presentations and talks](presentations.qmd)
:::
:::
::::

## Teaching, collaboration, and resources

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card .home-card}
### Collaboration

We work with researchers, institutions, and policy-facing partners on applied causal questions and infrastructure.

[Contact](mailto:joseph.bulbulia@vuw.ac.nz)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card .home-card}
### Supervision and teaching

The lab supports supervision and methods teaching in causal inference, psychology, and related social-science applications.

[Meet the lab](team.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.card .home-card}
### Resources

Presentations, practical resources, and recent news are available across the site.

[Browse resources](resources.qmd)
:::
:::
::::

## Research infrastructure

::: {.card .feature-card}
### Places Map

[Places Map](https://www.placesmap.org/) is ACCEPT Lab's open research infrastructure for studying places of worship and cultural context at scale. It supports reproducible spatial analysis, collaboration, and public scholarship.

::: {.feature-links}
[Visit Places Map](https://www.placesmap.org/)
[Code and workflows](https://github.com/go-bayes/places-of-worship)
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Recent activity
- [Places Map is live](news.qmd#places-map-is-live)
- [Recent talks, workshops, and slides](presentations.qmd)
- [Lab news and events](news.qmd)
:::

## Contact

If you would like to discuss collaboration, supervision, invited talks, or research infrastructure, please email.

[Email Joseph Bulbulia](mailto:joseph.bulbulia@vuw.ac.nz){.btn .btn-primary}
